body:
Supersedes #77 with the initial `delegate_issue` implementation plus the review follow-ups folded into a single clean commit.

## Summary
- keep `/orchestrate` as the public coordinator route while adding internal issue-level `delegate_issue` planner decisions
- create/reuse child issues with GitHub-visible parent/stage/state markers, deterministic parent child-link reuse, and terminal report-back that resumes the parent planner
- move delegated-route capability checks into orchestrator preflight and keep the dispatcher-side visible stop path
- harden child reuse/report state: strict `child_issue_number` parsing, same-repo closing refs, trusted agent/app-authored markers only, resumed-round failure comments, and narrower terminal state classification
- update orchestrator docs/prompt/workflow inputs and add regression coverage for the original review findings

## Validation
- `npm --prefix .agent test` (439 tests passed)
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `review` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777739498159 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNTY1MDM1OTI6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6ODI6cmV2aWV3OjgyOjI -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Adds `initialOrchestrateCapabilityStopReason()` and gates initial `/orchestrate` against `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr` capability before planner/dispatch; covered by preflight and handoff CLI tests. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Stop paths now create visible orchestration stop comments, including authorization stops and child delegation setup failures. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Planner contract uses explicit fields: `decision`, `child_stage`, `child_instructions`, `child_issue_number`, `base_branch`, and `base_pr`; ambiguous `base_branch` + `base_pr` is rejected. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Child issue reuse/setup failures are surfaced as parent comments and stop outputs; terminal report state uses pending/dispatched markers and regression tests cover reruns and malformed state. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, supported workflows, technical orchestrator docs, workflow inputs, and orchestrator prompt for `delegate_issue`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing access-policy helpers, handoff decision parsing, workflow dispatch helper, GitHub comment marker style, and review-summary minimization flow. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes stay scoped to orchestrator docs, prompt, workflow preflight, handoff CLI, sub-orchestration helpers, and focused tests. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The design keeps `/orchestrate` public, models `delegate_issue` as an internal decision, and stores parent/child state in issue markers rather than adding a broader route system. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers are strong authorization handling, visible stop reporting, strict child marker trust checks, and regression coverage for the review follow-ups.
- The PR also preserves the documented one-shot behavior for direct `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr`; the new multi-step behavior is limited to explicit `/orchestrate` meta-orchestration.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No blocking or warning findings against the selected applicable rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #82 supersedes #77 and implements the issue #76 design for issue-backed child orchestration.
- It keeps `/orchestrate` as the public coordinator route while adding internal `delegate_issue` planner decisions for issue-level meta-orchestration.
- Child issue state, parent links, terminal reports, and resumed parent planning are stored in GitHub-visible markers with trusted actor checks.
- The PR also folds in prior review follow-ups around authorization preflight, visible stop paths, same-repo closing refs, stricter child reuse, docs, prompt updates, and regression coverage.

## Review
The synthesized review outcome is clean: no reviewer raised a current blocking or required warning-level branch change. Claude noted a few design trade-offs and optional cleanups, while Codex and the rubrics review both concluded the current head is ready to ship.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | Independent reviews found no unresolved required branch-change work. Optional notes around terminal-state semantics, partial-access behavior, prompt clarity, and future extraction do not block merge. |

## Progress
- Prior #77 authorization-order, malformed child issue number, child setup visibility, cross-repo closing ref, marker trust, and stale docs concerns are reported fixed in #82.
- Current PR discussion already includes a passing rubrics review with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Existing inline comments on PR #82 are empty, and no new inline comments were posted because there are no concrete required line-specific findings.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved actionable branch work remains, and the remaining reviewer notes are optional design or maintenance observations.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #82.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted, non-duplicate durable preference in PR #82 that warranted a rubric edit. The PR conversation itself only had bot/agent comments, no inline comments, and no human reviews. Linked #77 context had a human concern about implementation cleanliness, but the reusable parts are already covered by active rubrics like `simple-implementation`, `reuse-existing-code`, `surgical-changes`, `keep-docs-in-sync`, and `surface-stateful-failures`.

Rubrics checkout remains clean.

title: Add delegate_issue child orchestration
